message: Update Cargo.lock and toast provider theme

files.patch:
@@ -157,7 +157,7 @@ {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.3","title":"Web: Enhanced Error States with Retry Buttons","description":"## Problem Error states show alert icon and message but no retry button. Users must manually refresh the entire page. This is poor UX - errors should be recoverable in-place. ## Solution Create reusable error state component with: 1. Error icon and message 2. Technical details (collapsible) 3. Retry button that re-fetches data 4. Helpful hints for common errors ## Implementation Details ```tsx // web/src/components/ui/error-state.tsx interface ErrorStateProps { error: Error | string onRetry?: () => void title?: string hint?: string } export function ErrorState({ error, onRetry, title, hint }: ErrorStateProps) { return (

{title || "Something went wrong"}

{String(error)}

{hint &&

{hint}

} {onRetry && ( )}
) } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - web/src/components/ui/error-state.tsx - new component - web/src/app/page.tsx - use ErrorState with mutate() - web/src/app/workers/page.tsx - use ErrorState - web/src/app/builds/page.tsx - use ErrorState - web/src/app/metrics/page.tsx - use ErrorState ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All pages use ErrorState component for errors - [ ] Retry button triggers data re-fetch - [ ] Helpful hints explain common issues - [ ] Error details collapsible for technical users ","notes":"## Testing Requirements ### Web E2E Tests (Playwright) ```typescript test('error state shows retry button', async ({ page }) => { // Mock API failure await page.route('**/api/status', route => route.fulfill({ status: 500 }) ); await page.goto('/'); // Error state visible await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="error-state"]')).toBeVisible(); await expect(page.getByText('Try Again')).toBeVisible(); }); test('retry button refetches data', async ({ page }) => { let callCount = 0; await page.route('**/api/status', route => { callCount++; if (callCount === 1) { route.fulfill({ status: 500 }); } else { route.fulfill({ json: mockStatus }); } }); await page.goto('/'); await page.click('text=Try Again'); // Data now visible await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="stat-card"]')).toBeVisible(); }); ``` ### Unit Tests (React Testing Library) - ErrorState renders with message - onRetry callback fires on click","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:40.153325416-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:47:22.774531335-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:47:22.774531335-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ErrorState component with retry functionality and integrated it into all 5 pages (dashboard, workers, builds, metrics, settings)","labels":["errors","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.3","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:40.181396235-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.4","title":"Web: Add Toast Notification System","description":"## Problem No user feedback for actions or state changes. When refresh completes or an action succeeds/fails, there's no indication. Premium apps provide toast notifications for user actions. ## Solution Implement toast notification system using Sonner (most popular React toast library): 1. Install sonner package 2. Add Toaster provider to layout 3. Use toast() for action feedback 4. Style toasts to match dark theme ## Usage Examples - "Data refreshed" on manual refresh - "Worker unreachable" when status changes to error - "Build completed" for active builds finishing - "Connection restored" after reconnecting ## Implementation ```tsx // web/src/app/layout.tsx import { Toaster } from 'sonner' ... // In components: import { toast } from 'sonner' toast.success('Data refreshed') toast.error('Failed to connect to daemon') ``` ## Files to Modify - package.json - add sonner dependency - web/src/app/layout.tsx - add Toaster - web/src/app/page.tsx - add refresh toast - web/src/app/workers/page.tsx - add action toasts ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Toast appears on manual refresh - [ ] Toast shows on error recovery - [ ] Toasts auto-dismiss after 4 seconds - [ ] Toasts match dark theme styling - [ ] Can dismiss toasts manually ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.512545526-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:22:45.146811025-05:00","labels":["feedback","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.4","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.583372542-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.5","title":"Web: Redesign Metrics Page for Non-DevOps Users","description":"## Problem Metrics page shows raw Prometheus text format in a pre tag. This is unhelpful for users who aren't DevOps engineers. Metrics should be visualized and explained. ## Current State - Raw text dump of Prometheus metrics - No explanation of what metrics mean - No visualization of trends - Budget section is good but sparse ## Solution Redesign metrics page with: 1. Key metrics as visual cards (similar to stat cards) 2. Mini sparklines for trends (if historical data available) 3. Expandable "raw metrics" section for power users 4. Metric explanations/tooltips ## Metrics to Highlight - Total compilations (counter) - Success rate (percentage gauge) - Average compilation time (with trend) - Active workers (current count) - Circuit breaker status (visual indicator) - Network transfer volume ## Implementation ```tsx
Raw Prometheus Metrics 
{metricsText}
``` ## Files to Modify - web/src/app/metrics/page.tsx - complete redesign - web/src/components/metrics/metric-card.tsx - new component - web/src/lib/api.ts - parse metrics into structured data ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Key metrics displayed as visual cards - [ ] Each metric has explanation tooltip - [ ] Raw metrics available but collapsed by default - [ ] Page is useful for non-technical users ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.276259325-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.276259325-05:00","labels":["metrics","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.5","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.471211673-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} -{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.6","title":"Web: Add Sorting and Filtering to Tables","description":"## Problem Build history and worker tables have no sorting or filtering. With many builds or workers, users can't find what they need quickly. Tables should be interactive. ## Solution Add to tables: 1. Column headers clickable for sorting (asc/desc) 2. Sort indicator icons 3. Filter input for searching 4. Pagination for large datasets ## Implementation Details - Use React state for sort column and direction - Client-side sorting (data already loaded) - Filter with debounced input - Visual sort indicators (▲/▼) ## Build History Table Filters - Status filter (success/failed/all) - Worker filter (dropdown) - Date range filter - Command search ## Workers Table Sorting - Sort by status (healthy first) - Sort by slot availability - Sort by speed score ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/builds/build-history-table.tsx - add sorting/filtering - web/src/components/workers/workers-grid.tsx - add sorting - web/src/components/ui/table.tsx - add sortable header component ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Click column header to sort - [ ] Sort direction indicator visible - [ ] Filter input filters table rows - [ ] Filters persist during polling updates - [ ] Pagination for >50 rows ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.799142181-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:22:40.955870877-05:00","labels":["tables","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.6","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.800559508-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} +{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.6","title":"Web: Add Sorting and Filtering to Tables","description":"## Problem Build history and worker tables have no sorting or filtering. With many builds or workers, users can't find what they need quickly. Tables should be interactive. ## Solution Add to tables: 1. Column headers clickable for sorting (asc/desc) 2. Sort indicator icons 3. Filter input for searching 4. Pagination for large datasets ## Implementation Details - Use React state for sort column and direction - Client-side sorting (data already loaded) - Filter with debounced input - Visual sort indicators (▲/▼) ## Build History Table Filters - Status filter (success/failed/all) - Worker filter (dropdown) - Date range filter - Command search ## Workers Table Sorting - Sort by status (healthy first) - Sort by slot availability - Sort by speed score ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/builds/build-history-table.tsx - add sorting/filtering - web/src/components/workers/workers-grid.tsx - add sorting - web/src/components/ui/table.tsx - add sortable header component ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Click column header to sort - [ ] Sort direction indicator visible - [ ] Filter input filters table rows - [ ] Filters persist during polling updates - [ ] Pagination for >50 rows ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.799142181-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:30:34.046792095-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T19:30:34.046792095-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented sorting, filtering, and pagination controls","labels":["tables","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.6","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.800559508-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","title":"Web: WCAG AA Accessibility Compliance","description":"## Problem Dashboard has accessibility gaps: - No aria-labels on custom components - Keyboard navigation not fully tested - Focus indicators could be stronger - Truncated content only accessible via hover ## Solution Audit and fix accessibility issues: 1. Add aria-labels to all interactive elements 2. Ensure all actions keyboard-accessible 3. Add visible focus rings 4. Make truncated content expandable 5. Add skip-to-main link ## Specific Fixes Needed - Sidebar nav: aria-current for active link - Refresh buttons: aria-label="Refresh data" - Progress bars: aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax - Tables: scope="col" on headers, aria-sort - Truncated commands: expandable on focus, not just hover - Color contrast: verify all text meets 4.5:1 ratio ## Testing - Tab through entire app - Test with VoiceOver/NVDA - Run Lighthouse accessibility audit - Use axe browser extension ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx - aria-current - web/src/components/ui/button.tsx - aria-label support - web/src/components/ui/progress.tsx - aria attributes - web/src/components/ui/table.tsx - scope, aria-sort - web/src/components/builds/build-history-table.tsx - expandable commands ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Lighthouse accessibility score > 90 - [ ] All interactive elements keyboard accessible - [ ] Screen reader announces all content - [ ] Focus visible on all interactive elements - [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1) ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.162061803-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:19:23.229486336-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:19:23.229486336-05:00","close_reason":"Added skip link, aria labels, progress/table accessibility, nav focus/aria-current","labels":["a11y","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.163335169-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.248582142-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:46.517116699-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:47.212590316-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.8","title":"Web: Add Dark/Light Mode Toggle","description":"## Problem Dashboard is dark-mode only. While dark mode is appropriate for developer tools, some users prefer light mode, especially in bright environments. The color scheme exists in globals.css but isn't toggleable. ## Solution Add theme toggle: 1. Theme toggle button in sidebar footer 2. Persist preference in localStorage 3. Respect system preference initially 4. Smooth transition between themes ## Implementation Use next-themes package: ```tsx // web/src/components/providers/theme-provider.tsx import { ThemeProvider } from 'next-themes' export function Providers({ children }) { return ( {children} ) } // Toggle button import { useTheme } from 'next-themes' const { theme, setTheme } = useTheme() ``` ## Files to Modify - package.json - add next-themes - web/src/app/layout.tsx - wrap with ThemeProvider - web/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx - add toggle button - web/src/app/globals.css - ensure light mode colors defined ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Toggle button visible in sidebar - [ ] Theme persists across sessions - [ ] System preference respected initially - [ ] Smooth transition animation - [ ] All components work in both themes ","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:16.595206452-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:39:03.170957611-05:00","labels":["theme","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.8","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:16.597366377-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-o9s","title":"Add toolchain field to protocol and transfer pipeline","description":"## Parent Epic: Automatic Toolchain Synchronization (remote_compilation_helper-ayn) ## Task Description Extend the RCH protocol to include toolchain information in selection requests and execution requests. The worker needs to know which toolchain to use for compilation. ## Changes Required ### 1. Update SelectionRequest ```rust // In rch-common/src/protocol.rs #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct SelectionRequest { pub project_id: String, pub required_cores: u32, pub preferred_workers: Vec, pub toolchain: Option, // NEW } ``` ### 2. Update Daemon API Parsing ```rust // In rchd/src/api.rs // Parse toolchain from query params or body fn parse_selection_request(request: &Request) -> Result { // ... existing parsing ... // Parse toolchain if provided let toolchain = query.get("toolchain") .map(|s| serde_json::from_str(s)) .transpose()?; Ok(SelectionRequest { // ... existing fields ... toolchain, }) } ``` ### 3. Update ExecutionRequest (Worker Protocol) ```rust // In rch-common/src/protocol.rs or worker protocol #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct ExecutionRequest { pub command: String, pub working_dir: PathBuf, pub env: HashMap, pub toolchain: Option, // NEW } ``` ### 4. Update Transfer Pipeline ```rust // In rch/src/transfer.rs impl TransferPipeline { /// Execute command on remote worker with toolchain pub async fn execute_remote( &self, worker: &WorkerConfig, command: &str, toolchain: Option<&ToolchainInfo>, ) -> Result { let wrapped_command = match toolchain { Some(tc) => format!( "rustup run {} {}", tc.rustup_toolchain(), command ), None => command.to_string(), }; self.ssh_client.execute(&wrapped_command).await } } ``` ### 5. Update Hook to Pass Toolchain ```rust // In rch/src/hook.rs async fn handle_compilation(command: &str, project_root: &Path) -> HookDecision { // Detect toolchain let toolchain = detect_toolchain(project_root).ok(); // Include in selection request let request = SelectionRequest { project_id: project_id.clone(), required_cores: estimate_cores(command), preferred_workers: vec![], toolchain: toolchain.clone(), }; // ... query daemon ... // Include in execution let result = pipeline.execute_remote( &worker, command, toolchain.as_ref(), ).await; } ``` ## Protocol Wire Format The toolchain can be sent as: 1. Query parameter (URL-encoded JSON) 2. Request body (for POST requests) 3. Custom header Recommended: URL-encoded JSON in query param for GET, body for POST. ``` GET /select-worker?project=foo&cores=4&toolchain=%7B%22channel%22%3A%22nightly%22%2C%22date%22%3A%222024-01-15%22%7D ``` Or cleaner with POST body: ```json { "project_id": "foo", "required_cores": 4, "toolchain": { "channel": "nightly", "date": "2024-01-15" } } ``` ## Files to Modify - `rch-common/src/protocol.rs` or `rch-common/src/types.rs` - `rchd/src/api.rs` - `rch/src/hook.rs` - `rch/src/transfer.rs` ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_selection_request_with_toolchain() { let request = SelectionRequest { project_id: "test".to_string(), required_cores: 4, preferred_workers: vec![], toolchain: Some(ToolchainInfo { channel: "nightly".to_string(), date: Some("2024-01-15".to_string()), full_version: "nightly-2024-01-15".to_string(), }), }; let json = serde_json::to_string(&request).unwrap(); let parsed: SelectionRequest = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap(); assert_eq!(parsed.toolchain.unwrap().channel, "nightly"); } #[test] fn test_command_wrapping_with_toolchain() { let tc = ToolchainInfo { channel: "nightly".to_string(), date: Some("2024-01-15".to_string()), full_version: "".to_string(), }; let wrapped = wrap_command("cargo build", Some(&tc)); assert_eq!(wrapped, "rustup run nightly-2024-01-15 cargo build"); } #[test] fn test_command_no_wrapping_without_toolchain() { let wrapped = wrap_command("cargo build", None); assert_eq!(wrapped, "cargo build"); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] SelectionRequest includes toolchain field - [ ] ExecutionRequest includes toolchain field - [ ] Daemon API parses toolchain from requests - [ ] Transfer pipeline wraps commands with rustup run - [ ] Hook detects and passes toolchain through pipeline - [ ] Serialization/deserialization works correctly - [ ] Tests cover protocol changes ## Dependencies - Requires: "Implement local toolchain version detection" task ## Estimated Effort: 2-3 hours","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:12:59.322422438-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:28:30.91001043-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:28:30.91001043-05:00","close_reason":"Protocol updated: toolchain field added to SelectionRequest, transfer pipeline supports toolchain wrapping via wrap_command_with_toolchain, hook interface updated. Full integration with detect_toolchain pending.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-o9s","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-6qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:14:48.463999007-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
